name: Patch Mono
explanation: An original semi-modular monophonic synth voice: two slightly detuned oscillators (saw plus a triangle-flavoured pulse, with a ring-mod blend in the middle) feed an aggressive two-stage resonant filter — a state-variable high-pass into a Sallen-Key-style low-pass that can self-oscillate and scream. An ADSR drives the amplitude and (by EnvAmount) the low-pass cutoff, with a touch of pre-filter drive. Last-note priority.
